source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design a transverse watertight bulkhead panel representative of a naval-engineering research benchmark for a 30-meter aluminum high-speed patrol craft, sized per ABS Rules for Building and Classing High-Speed Craft (Part 3), as a welded multi-part aluminum panel assembly consisting of the 6 mm 5083-H116 plate, five vertical T-bar stiffeners (6 mm x 75 mm flat-bar on 600 mm pitch across 2.8 m panel width), two horizontal edge frames at top and bottom of panel, two vertical edge frames at port/starboard tying into the longitudinal bulkheads, and the stiffener-to-plate / frame-to-plate fillet welds. Panel outer dimensions 2.2 m height x 2.8 m width, mounted between two longitudinal bulkheads and the hull plating on each side, 4.0 m above the design waterline. Material: marine-grade AL 5083-H116 (Young's modulus 70.3 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 228 MPa, ultimate 317 MPa, density 2660 kg/m^3). Plate thickness 6 mm with vertical stiffeners on 600 mm pitch, TB-section flat-bar 6 mm x 75 mm. Load cases: (LC1) hydrostatic head at flooding case: 4 m water column = 40 kPa on panel; (LC2) slamming pressure per ABS HSC Part 3: 60 kPa (at panel station 4 m above WL) applied as dynamic-equivalent static; (LC3) naval grade-B shock equivalent 10 g vertical + 6 g athwartship simultaneous (MIL-S-901D analog representation); (LC4) fire-safety deformation limit: panel must survive compartment fire with thermal gradient 100 C across panel for 30 minutes without exceeding 0.5% elastic strain; (LC5) fatigue: 10^7 cycles of +/- 10 kPa wave-induced pressure at 0.1 Hz; (LC6) anti-shrapnel load: 50 kN point load on a 100 mm x 100 mm patch simulating blast fragment. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises panel + stiffeners under LC1-LC3 <= 152 MPa (yield 228 / 1.5); (R2) panel mid-span out-of-plane deflection under LC2 <= panel_width / 200 = 14 mm; (R3) shock LC3 no yield, first natural mode of panel above 30 Hz; (R4) fatigue alternating stress at stiffener toe welds <= 40 MPa (BS 7608 Class F at 1e7 cycles); (R5) panel mass <= 180 kg; (R6) linear buckling eigenvalue for panel under in-plane compression from deck loads >= 3.0. Verification is performed on the assembled plate + stiffeners + edge frames welded model with representative BS 7608 Class F weld stiffness; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, fatigue summary, buckling eigenvalue, modal, and mass report.
short_prompt:
Design a transverse watertight bulkhead panel representative of a naval-engineering research benchmark for a 30-meter aluminum high-speed patrol craft, sized per ABS Rules for Building and Classing High-Speed Craft (Part 3).

Panel outer dimensions 2.2 m height x 2.8 m width, mounted between two longitudinal bulkheads and the hull plating, 4.0 m above the design waterline. Material: marine-grade AL 5083-H116 (Young's modulus 70.3 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 228 MPa, ultimate 317 MPa, density 2660 kg/m^3). Plate thickness 6 mm with vertical stiffeners on 600 mm pitch, TB-section flat-bar 6 mm x 75 mm.

Load cases: LC1 hydrostatic head at flooding case: 4 m water column = 40 kPa on panel; LC2 slamming pressure per ABS HSC Part 3: 60 kPa at panel station 4 m above WL; LC3 naval grade-B shock equivalent 10 g vertical + 6 g athwartship simultaneous (MIL-S-901D analog); LC4 fire-safety thermal gradient 100 C across panel for 30 minutes; LC5 fatigue 10^7 cycles of +/- 10 kPa wave-induced pressure at 0.1 Hz; LC6 anti-shrapnel load 50 kN point load on a 100 mm x 100 mm patch.

prompt.title: Naval aluminum bulkhead panel (multi-part welded assembly)
prompt.brief: 5083-H116 watertight bulkhead: 6 mm plate + 5 vertical T-bar stiffeners + 2 horizontal edge frames + 2 vertical edge frames, 2.2 m x 2.8 m panel.

parts.name: bulkhead_plate
parts.role: 6 mm 5083-H116 plate forming the watertight bulkhead face

parts.name: vertical_tbar_stiffener
parts.role: vertical T-bar flat-bar stiffener, 6 mm web x 75 mm depth, at 600 mm pitch

parts.name: horizontal_edge_frame
parts.role: top / bottom horizontal edge frame tying the panel into the deck / tank top

parts.name: vertical_edge_frame
parts.role: port / starboard vertical edge frame tying the panel into the longitudinal bulkheads

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 2.2 m H x 2.8 m W x ~100 mm D (including stiffener depth)
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X ship-longitudinal (panel normal), Y athwartship, Z vertical

boundary_conditions.interfaces: vertical T-bar stiffeners fillet-welded to plate at BS 7608 Class F detail; horizontal and vertical edge frames fillet-welded to plate; edge frames welded into longitudinal bulkheads and deck / tank top
boundary_conditions.support: edge frames fixed at longitudinal bulkhead / deck / tank-top boundaries in all LCs

prompt.objective: minimize panel mass under 180 kg while passing yield, deflection, modal, fatigue, and buckling gates

interfaces.load_path: vertical T-bar stiffeners fillet-welded to plate (BS 7608 Class F detail)
top / bottom horizontal edge frames welded to plate
port / starboard vertical edge frames welded to plate
stiffener ends welded into horizontal edge frames

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: ABS-HSC aluminum bulkhead panels are stiffened plates with welded vertical stiffeners and bounding frames; fatigue and shock gates explicitly target weld details at stiffener toe and edge frames, requiring a multi-body welded model.
notes.speculation_caveats: Stiffener count (5) and edge-frame layout are speculative; plate material, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.
